SUBJECTIVE:
Diarrhoea since yesterday morning, first noted about 6am yesterday, 6-7 episodes yesterday and 3 this morning. Stool watery with mucus, tiny red streak once last night. One vomit yesterday afternoon, yellow/foamy, none overnight. Appetite lower, drinking slightly more, slightly quieter but still waggy/wanting to go out, urgency overnight with no house accident.

OBJECTIVE:
Bright, responsive, friendly, slightly quieter than typical. Wt 28.4 kg. T 38.7C, HR 108, RR 28. MM pink/moist. CRT<2. Mild cranial abdo discomfort on deep palpation, no obvious mass, no severe guarding.

ASSESSMENT:
Acute gastroenteritis, likely dietary indiscretion.

PLAN:
Monitor for repeated vomiting, unable to keep water down, increasing blood in stool, marked lethargy, abdominal pain, or no improvement by 48h. Nurse progress call tomorrow at 15:30. If still loose/not improving, recheck and faecal testing. Maropitant 1 mg/kg SC in clinic. Pro-Kolin 5 ml PO q8h x3 days.
